rationale: The homes in this Spring, TX subdivision display classic hip-roof geometry. Using the 50-ft scale bar, ground shadows cast from the eave edges measure approximately 9–12 feet, consistent with a moderate 6:12 pitch given typical 8–10 ft eave heights and the apparent sun angle. The hip-roof ridges are clearly set inward from the building footprint, with the visible roof plane occupying roughly 30–40% of each structure's plan width — too prominent for a 4:12 but not foreshortened enough for 8:12+. Roof plane texture and shadow-edge definition confirm meaningful slope without extreme steepness. Regional context (Houston suburban construction, late 1990s–early 2000s era, warm southern climate) further supports a 6:12 dominant pitch as the most probable estimate.
